questions.question_text: Which license authorizes managing rental property for others for compensation in Arkansas?
questions.correct_answer: A broker's license or a property management broker license
distractors: A salesperson's license held independently
A general business license only
No license is required
questions.explanation: Property management for compensation requires an AREC license: the traditional path through a broker's license, or, since Act 392 of 2025, a standalone property management broker license. Salespersons and PM associates may perform management activities only under supervising-broker authority, never independently.

questions.question_text: Which of the following property management activities requires an AREC license?
distractors: Mowing the lawn for a property owner
Scheduling maintenance for your own rental
Painting a unit between tenants
questions.explanation: Leasing, renting, collecting rent, listing property for rent, and negotiating leases for compensation all require an AREC license (a broker's license or, since Act 392 of 2025, a property management license). Physical maintenance does not.

questions.question_text: A property management company negotiates lease terms between landlords and tenants. The company must hold:
questions.correct_answer: A broker or property management broker license
distractors: Only a general business license
A federal property management certification
A salesperson's license held independently
questions.explanation: Negotiating lease transactions for compensation requires broker-level licensure: a real estate broker's license or, since Act 392 of 2025, a property management broker license. A salesperson-level license alone is not sufficient to run a property management business.

questions.question_text: A salesperson wants to manage rental properties for compensation. How can they lawfully do this?
questions.correct_answer: Under the supervision of their principal or executive broker
distractors: Independently, under their salesperson license
Only after passing the broker exam
They cannot participate in property management at all
questions.explanation: A salesperson may perform property management activities under principal or executive broker supervision, never independently. Alternatively, Act 392 of 2025 created property management broker and associate licenses for PM-only practice.

questions.question_text: Since Act 392 of 2025, which property-management-specific licenses does Arkansas issue?
questions.correct_answer: Property management broker and property management associate
distractors: A property management endorsement on a salesperson license
A federal property management certification
None; only full broker licenses exist
questions.explanation: Act 392 of 2025 created two PM-specific licenses: the property management broker, who may run a leasing and rental business but not sell real estate, and the property management associate, who works under a PM broker much like a salesperson works under a principal broker.

questions.question_text: Which of the following persons is exempt from needing a PM license?
questions.correct_answer: An on-site manager who lives at the property
distractors: A friend collecting rent as a favor for pay
A real estate salesperson managing rentals
An investor managing others' rental properties
questions.explanation: On-site residential managers who reside at the property and manage as part of their employment are exempt. Property owners managing their own properties are also exempt.

questions.question_text: A landlord manages their own 10-unit apartment building. Do they need a license?
questions.correct_answer: No, owners managing their own property are exempt
distractors: Yes, for any building over 4 units
Yes, if they collect rent from tenants
Only if they also list units for rent
questions.explanation: Property owners managing their own properties are exempt from licensing requirements regardless of the number of units.

questions.question_text: An on-site manager lives in Unit 1 and manages the other 11 units. They move to a different building but continue managing. Are they still exempt?
questions.correct_answer: No, the exemption requires residing at the property
distractors: Yes, once granted the exemption is permanent
Yes, for up to 12 months after moving
Yes, if they visit the property daily
questions.explanation: The on-site manager exemption requires the manager to reside at the property. Once they no longer live on-site, the exemption no longer applies and a license is needed.

questions.question_text: A retired real estate agent manages a friend's rental properties for a flat monthly fee. Does this require a license?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, managing for compensation requires a license
distractors: No, since they are managing for a friend
No, for retired agents with 10+ years experience
Only if they are listed as the property's agent
questions.explanation: Managing rental properties for compensation (including a flat fee) requires a broker's license. Personal relationships do not create an exemption.

questions.question_text: Which statement about Arkansas PM exemptions is correct?
questions.correct_answer: Both owners and on-site resident managers are exempt
distractors: Only property owners are exempt
Only licensed attorneys are exempt
There are no exemptions for property management
questions.explanation: Arkansas exempts two groups from PM licensing: property owners managing their own properties and on-site residential managers who reside at the property as part of their employment.

questions.question_text: A property manager must maintain client rental income in:
distractors: Their personal checking account
The property owner's bank account directly
A general operating account
questions.explanation: Property managers must maintain separate trust accounts for client funds, just like sales brokers. Commingling with personal or operating funds is prohibited.

questions.question_text: What must a written Arkansas property management agreement state?
distractors: Notarization by a notary public
A filing number issued by AREC
A minimum two-year term
questions.explanation: Like other written agency agreements, property management agreements must state a definite expiration date. They do not need notarization, AREC filing, or any minimum term.

questions.question_text: A property manager's relationship to the property owner is BEST described as:
distractors: An independent contractor relationship only
A casual business arrangement
A landlord-tenant relationship
questions.explanation: Property managers must act as fiduciaries for property owners, meaning they must put the owner's interests first and handle funds with the highest standard of care.

questions.question_text: A property manager must provide property owners with:
distractors: Daily phone call updates
Tenant credit reports upon request only
Reports only at the end of the lease term
questions.explanation: Property managers must provide regular financial reports to property owners as part of their fiduciary duties and the terms of the management agreement.

questions.question_text: Property managers in Arkansas must comply with which additional firm requirements?
distractors: Only trust account requirements
No requirements beyond their PM agreement
Only the annual CE requirement
questions.explanation: Property managers must comply with all licensing requirements including advertising rules, supervision requirements, trust account regulations, and CE — the same as sales brokers.
